title: Mitotically heritable effects of BMAA on striatal neural stem cell proliferation and differentiation.
summary: An in vitro laboratory study investigating the direct and mitotically inherited effects of the cyanobacterial toxin BMAA on neural stem cell (NSC) proliferation, differentiation, and morphology.
key_findings: Neural stem cells are more susceptible to BMAA than primary neurons.
250 µM BMAA reduces NSC proliferation through apoptosis and G2/M arrest.
50-100 µM BMAA reduces differentiation of NSCs into astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, and neurons.
BMAA-induced changes are mitotically heritable to daughter cells.
population: Embryonic neural stem cells and primary striatal neurons
limitations: In vitro study, not human epidemiological data
